message:
beads: sync issue tracker — wa-1u90p.1.1 progress update

Updated wa-1u90p.1.1 (resize baseline scenarios) with implementation
progress comment from IndigoMountain documenting simulation layer updates,
fixture creation, and quality gate status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1u8k","title":"CLI: set pane priority at runtime","description":"## What
Add a command to set pane priority/weights without restarting.

## Why
Operators need to react to incidents in real time.

## How
- CLI: `wa panes priority <pane_id> --weight N`
- Persist override in storage or in-memory registry with TTL

## Success Criteria
- Priority changes take effect immediately
- Overrides are visible via status/health output","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:00.210578659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:32.039776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:32.039780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p","title":"[EPIC] Zero-Hitch Resize/Reflow Rewrite for WezTerm Mux (60+ FPS, crash-free, smart wrapping)","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm inherits severe resize/font-change instability and pathological latency from current WezTerm/mux resize behavior under large scrollback and multi-pane/tab concurrency. Observed failure modes include long stalls, visual artifacts (stretched text before settle), and occasional crashes.

## Objective
Redesign the full resize/reflow pipeline so user-perceived behavior is effectively instantaneous, artifact-free, and robust under heavy load. Target sustained smoothness at 60+ FPS where hardware allows, with graceful degradation and deterministic behavior when hardware is constrained.

## Product Goals
- Near-zero hitching on resize and font changes
- No stretched/garbled intermediate frames
- Stable behavior with huge scrollback and many panes/tabs
- Intelligent line-breaking quality for terminal text (monospace-adapted Knuth-Plass + pragmatic fallbacks)
- Full backwards compatibility with existing terminal semantics and safety constraints

## Engineering Goals
- Formalized latency budgets, frame budgets, and correctness invariants
- Coalesced/asynchronous resize control plane (latest intent wins)
- Incremental viewport-first reflow and background completion
- GPU-first rendering/presentation strategy with robust CPU fallback parity
- Comprehensive test matrix: unit, property, integration, e2e, perf, soak, failure-injection

## Non-Negotiables
- No feature regressions in terminal behavior
- No architecture that relies on best-effort timing heuristics alone
- No rollout without measurable pass/fail gates

## Deliverables
A complete multi-track implementation and validation program covering architecture, algorithms, rendering, mux concurrency, reliability hardening, and production rollout controls.

## Success Criteria
- All track epics complete with objective evidence and no unresolved high-risk blocker.
- SLO and quality gates are satisfied across correctness, latency, artifact suppression, and stability.
- Rollout readiness (flags, dashboards, runbooks, rollback) is demonstrated before go/no-go sign-off.","design":"Two-phase resize architecture (interactive + background completion), latest-intent coalescing, artifact-free atomic presentation, and GPU-first with deterministic CPU parity.","acceptance_criteria":"All track epics complete with objective SLO gates passed; full test matrix green; rollout package approved; final go/no-go evidence accepted.","notes":"Any major design change must include benchmark evidence and explicit regression-risk analysis in bead comments.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.052067Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:22:39.519394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","gpu","mux","performance","reflow","resize","ux"],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Master Execution Blueprint: Zero-Hitch Resize/Reflow

## Problem statement (user-facing)
Current resize/font-change behavior under mux + large scrollback can exhibit:
- long interactive stalls
- visual corruption/transient stretched text
- lock-amplified latency across panes/tabs
- crash risk under resize storms

This epic exists to make resize behavior feel immediate and stable in real-world heavy workloads while preserving terminal correctness.

## Architectural thesis
1. **Control-plane first**: explicit resize transaction lifecycle with latest-intent semantics.
2. **Viewport-first convergence**: interactive phase prioritizes visible work under strict budget; background phase completes cold scrollback.
3. **Artifact-free presentation**: never present incoherent intermediate frames.
4. **Algorithmic quality under budget**: monospace-adapted Knuth-Plass where feasible, deterministic fallback when budget pressure rises.
5. **Reliability over heroics**: enforce invariants, detect stalls, degrade gracefully, and preserve interactivity.
6. **Evidence-driven rollout**: no release without objective SLO gates and reproducible validation artifacts.

## Global success metrics
- No catastrophic interaction stalls during resize/font churn
- No stretched-text artifact class in e2e validation
- Crash budget effectively zero for resize paths in soak + fault-injection runs
- Deterministic correctness for Unicode width/cursor invariants
- CI catches perf regressions before merge

## Dependency logic
- Baseline forensics (Track 1) must establish bottleneck truth before architectural commitments.
- Control plane (Track 2) enables safe orchestration for layout/render/mux tracks.
- Layout/render/mux tracks execute in parallel after control-plane foundations stabilize.
- Reliability and validation tracks formalize correctness and operational confidence.
- Rollout track operationalizes compatibility contracts, canaries, alerts, and rollback.

## Testing doctrine (non-negotiable)
- Unit + property + integration + e2e + perf + soak are required.
- Every test domain must emit machine-readable diagnostics.
- Performance gates must be enforced in CI with explicit thresholds.
- Regression triage must be possible from artifacts alone (no manual guesswork).

## Scope constraints
- No feature loss in terminal semantics.
- No hidden coupling that requires undocumented “timing luck”.
- No rollout based on anecdotal performance impressions.

## Notes for future implementers
- Preserve comparability: don’t change benchmark harnesses and thresholds silently.
- Every major optimization should carry before/after measurements and risk notes.
- If quality-vs-latency tradeoffs are introduced, document exact fallback criteria.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:44Z"},{"id":53,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Plan-Space Optimization Review (v2)

Performed a full second-pass audit of all `wa-1u90p*` beads for:
- dependency topology and critical-path quality
- user-perceived responsiveness safeguards
- validation completeness (unit/integration/e2e/perf/soak)
- logging/artifact quality for rapid regression triage

## Key revisions applied
1. Added user-critical tasks for interaction continuity and perception:
- `wa-1u90p.2.7` input-first scheduler guardrails
- `wa-1u90p.7.8` input-latency e2e harness
- `wa-1u90p.7.9` automated visual artifact detector
- `wa-1u90p.7.7` alt-screen app conformance e2e
- `wa-1u90p.8.7` controlled beta feedback + telemetry loop

2. Added quality rigor tasks for layout/render adaptation:
- `wa-1u90p.3.7` wrap-quality corpus and scorecard
- `wa-1u90p.4.7` adaptive rendering quality policy by hardware tier

3. Tightened release gate dependencies:
- Final go/no-go (`wa-1u90p.8.6`) now explicitly depends on new high-signal e2e/perception tasks.

4. Strengthened critical gate descriptions:
- `wa-1u90p.1.4`, `wa-1u90p.2.1`, `wa-1u90p.7.4`, `wa-1u90p.8.1`, `wa-1u90p.8.6` now have explicit measurable acceptance definitions.

5. Improved validation observability requirements:
- Validation tasks now require structured correlation IDs, per-stage latency fields, and attached machine-readable evidence artifacts.

## Current quality status
- `br lint` on `wa-1u90p*`: 0 warnings
- cycle check on `wa-1u90p*`: 0 cycles

This revision preserves original scope/functionality and increases user-outcome confidence before implementation.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1","title":"Baseline Forensics, SLOs, and Bottleneck Attribution","description":"Establish reproducible worst-case workloads, hard performance SLOs, and evidence-based bottleneck attribution before deeper architectural changes.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Baseline-first evidence track","acceptance_criteria":"All baseline artifacts complete and approved","notes":"Downstream tracks depend on this evidence","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.159442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:43.308530Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","performance","reflow","resize","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.159442Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 1 Detailed Intent: Baseline Forensics and SLO Grounding

This track creates the empirical foundation for the entire program.

## Why this track exists
Without reproducible scenarios and stage-level attribution, optimization effort drifts into guesswork. This track forces a measurable starting point.

## Required outputs
- deterministic stress scenarios
- resize timeline instrumentation (with queue/phase visibility)
- lock/memory profiling under storm load
- SLO/quality-gate document
- ranked bottleneck dossier
- frankentui reuse matrix (Knuth-Plass, scratch buffers, virtualization ideas)

## What “done” means
Any downstream implementation task should be able to point to this track’s artifacts and justify why it is expected to move specific metrics.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","title":"Define hard SLOs: frame-time, interaction latency, artifact tolerance, and crash budget","description":"## Context
All later implementation and rollout decisions depend on explicit, measurable targets that reflect user perception and operational reliability.

## Scope
Define the authoritative SLO/SLA-style gate set for resize/reflow work, including frame pacing, interaction latency, artifact classes, and crash budgets.

## Deliverables
- SLO specification with p50/p95/p99 thresholds per workload class
- Hardware-tier matrix (low/mid/high) with expected behavior envelopes
- Pass/fail gate definitions for CI, soak, and go/no-go review
- Exception/degradation policy with trigger conditions and recovery criteria

## Why this matters
Without hard targets, optimization becomes subjective and release risk increases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SLO document defines explicit numeric thresholds for frame-time, interaction latency, artifact incidence, and crash-free operation.
- Thresholds are mapped to specific benchmark/e2e harnesses and artifact outputs.
- Low/mid/high hardware tier expectations are explicit and testable.
- Degradation/rollback triggers are documented and referenced by rollout beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:53:49.592084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reflow","resize","slo","track-baseline","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.693879Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.798467Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.2","title":"Resize Control Plane: Coalescing, Scheduling, and State Machine","description":"Implement an explicit resize control plane with latest-intent semantics, bounded work scheduling, and deterministic state transitions.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Explicit resize control plane","acceptance_criteria":"Coalescing, scheduler, and overload controls validated","notes":"No stale-intent backlog allowed","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.269560Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:43.440109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","epic","reflow","resize","track-control-plane"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.269560Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:27.530331Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 2 Detailed Intent: Resize Control Plane

## Core requirement
Resize must be orchestrated through an explicit transaction model, not ad-hoc nested calls.

## Critical properties
- latest-intent wins
- stale-intent cancellation safety
- bounded queue growth
- frame-budget-aware scheduling
- introspectable lifecycle telemetry

## Failure handling
Overload/backpressure policy must preserve interaction even when quality work is deferred.

## Deliverable quality bar
Must be debuggable in production: operators need to see why work was deferred/cancelled/committed.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","title":"Author ADR for two-phase resize transactions and explicit state machine","description":"## Context
A robust resize architecture requires explicit transaction semantics and lifecycle invariants.

## Scope
Author the ADR for two-phase resize transactions, coalescing policy, cancellation behavior, and scheduler interaction contracts.

## Deliverables
- ADR with state diagram and transition guards
- Definition of latest-intent semantics and stale-work cancellation rules
- Failure-mode analysis (deadlock, starvation, queue overload) and mitigations
- Migration plan from current behavior to new control-plane behavior

## Why this matters
This bead is the architectural contract that prevents ad-hoc behavior drift across tracks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADR includes complete state machine, invariants, and edge-case handling.
- Decision alternatives and tradeoffs are documented with rationale.
- At least one simulation/test fixture validates state transitions and cancellation semantics.
